name: eleutherococcus senticosus
mechanism: Eleutherosides and phenylpropanoid glycosides show stress-response modulation and may influence HPA-axis signaling and fatigue-related outcomes.
contraindications: Use caution with prescription medications and chronic conditions; monitor for intolerance.
safetyNotes: Usually tolerated short term, but may cause insomnia, palpitations, or blood-pressure changes at higher doses. Avoid use in uncontrolled hypertension.

sources.title: Eleutherococcus senticosus (Siberian ginseng): safety and efficacy review (Am Fam Physician. 2003)
sources.note: Clinical safety considerations and evidence limits.

interactions: May increase or decrease blood glucose; monitor when combined with antidiabetic medicines.
Possible interaction with digoxin immunoassays and caution with anticoagulants based on case-level reports.
May increase stimulation with caffeine or other stimulants in sensitive users.
therapeuticUses: traditional support for mild digestive comfort
sideEffects: mild gastrointestinal upset (nausea, cramping, or loose stool)
allergic rash or itching in sensitive individuals
